rows.notes: Clear page title centered at top. Legible handwriting and no monetary figures on this row. Vertical ruling lines not needed for this header.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Balance of rent received from Mr Jackson covering March 1765 to March 1768, amounting to £103 14s 11d.

rows.english_description_with_amount: Midwinter gift received from Benson in 1767, amounting to £60 12s 4d.

rows.notes: A number-only row immediately under a short ruling line; appears to be a subtotal or carried figure. The digits '340-7-3' are clear and align with the pounds/shillings/pence columns. No description present on the ledger line itself.
Number-only subtotal '126-10-0' clearly written and well aligned. No descriptive text on the left.
Number-only subtotal '154-10-0' clearly visible and columns unambiguous.
Number-only subtotal '105-2-4' clearly written. Pence and shilling values are within valid range.

Heavier underlined total appears beneath the group of subtotals. The reading '726-9-11' is visible though slightly faded; alignment with column rulings used to confirm digit boundaries. There is some faintness in the pence digit which reduces confidence slightly.

rows.description: In Manner ex Deb: an: Precedentij
rows.notes: Description is a Latinized phrase (handwritten as 'In Manner ex Deb: an: Precedentij'); readable but slightly stylized. Amount reads as '195-10-4 1/2' — the halfpenny symbol (loop after pence) is visible and interpreted as 0.5. Column alignment supports the reading. Minor uncertainty in the final fractional mark's clarity led to lowered confidence.
rows.english_description: Receipt in settlement of a debt from the preceding year.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt in settlement of a debt from the preceding year, amounting to £195 10s 4½d.

rows.notes: Final summed line reads 'Summa Receptorum' (heading for total receipts) with amount that arithmetically corresponds to adding the heavy total (726-9-11) and the prior line (195-10-4 1/2) => 922-0-3 1/2. The pence fraction is interpreted as a halfpenny (0.5) from the small loop after the pence figure. The final fraction/last pence digit in the scan is slightly faint, so confidence is moderate but consistent with column arithmetic.
